selectionManifest.canonicalContentHashRule: sha256 over canonical JSON of the create object only

create.statement: All six built-in contracts resolve in deterministic order and decode distinct bounded request/result shapes.
Requests bind WorkflowId, Generation, stage identity, exact ordered source bytes and hashes, repository target, and revision intent where applicable.
Changed, reordered, duplicate, malformed, missing, or oversized sources and results are rejected at Schema or persistence boundaries.
Ticket and accepted-artifact precedence is deterministic and covered by tests.
A test contract proves extension without stage-specific orchestrator changes.
Individual limits are tested without claiming aggregate storage capacity.

create.title: Design decision D1
create.position: Register six built-in contracts in explicit Questions-to-Implementation order, each with a distinct stage-tagged request/result Schema and document or implementation projection
create.rationale: Preserve stage meaning and deterministic extension without a central stage switch

create.title: Design decision D2
create.position: Build one ordered exact-source envelope with Ticket reference first, enabled accepted technical predecessors newest-to-oldest, exact scope/definition/target identities, and contract-local fields
create.rationale: Bind every request to complete accepted authority without false shared payload semantics

create.title: Design decision D3
create.position: Before assembly, compare repository/workflow/Generation/role/revision/final-artifact authority; then read by exact commit/path under a byte cap and verify observed commit, path, blob SHA, and content SHA-256
create.rationale: Prevent valid Git content from being substituted across repository, workflow, stage, or accepted revision

create.title: Design decision D4
create.position: Keep heterogeneous closure storage and type erasure inside `TrustedStageCatalog`; select one trusted registration, decode/bound request and result with its Schemas, invoke only its closures, and decode prepared output
create.rationale: Restore exact concrete types at one trusted seam and avoid generic-runner stage switches

create.title: Design decision D5
create.position: Encode versioned `StageProduceInput` with exact scope, contract ref, complete decoded request, and canonical request hash; load and rehash the exact content-addressed ticket row; replay persisted technical bytes without mutable rediscovery
create.rationale: Make retry/restart deterministic while keeping every valid stage request within 32 KiB

create.title: Design decision D6
create.position: Apply per-read, per-source UTF-8, complete request, configured input, contract-result, prompt/launch, and global result bounds; reject malformed, mistagged, changed, or oversized values before a downstream crossing
create.rationale: Bound resource use and stop malformed authority or output at the lowest owned boundary

create.title: Design decision D7
create.position: CAP-D2 supplies pure currentness expectations, comparison inputs, and typed mismatch diagnostics; CAP-D3/D7 atomically persist/transition only matching work, and CAP-D3/D4/D7 repeat claim/task-exposure fences
create.rationale: Ensure canonical hashes prove immutability without being mistaken for current authority

create.title: Design decision D8
create.position: Prove six-contract order and a seventh registration-only extension; test exact limits, wrong-scope/hash corruption, immutable reads, ticket rows, and outputs locally while leaving transition races and execution/publication/gate/Provenance/capacity lifecycles to their owners
create.rationale: Supply proportionate evidence without claiming or implementing neighboring lifecycles

create.name: R1 control C1
create.statement: Derive predecessor membership/order from trusted snapshots and accepted pointers; check repository, Workflow, Generation, role/stage, accepted revision, and complete final artifact before reads; verify observed commit/path/blob/content after capped reads.

create.name: R1 control C2
create.statement: Return bounded typed source role/index and stable reasons for authority, observation, order, duplicate, malformed, and size failures.

create.name: R1 control C3
create.description: Owner `workflowd-vs3.4.2`; phase Before exposure; verification V1
create.statement: Return no encoded input or task until every expected source validates and the ordered reference-array source hash recomputes.

create.name: R1 control C4
create.description: Owner `workflowd-vs3.4.7`; phase Recovery; verification V2; ship after C1-C3 and before successor exposure
create.statement: Correct/release accepted pointers only through guarded progression; retry immutable assembly without redirecting old references.

create.name: R2 control C5
create.description: Owner `workflowd-vs3.4.3`; phase Before exposure; verification V4; ship after CAP-D2 codecs and before claims
create.statement: In the ready transition, atomically compare Generation, snapshots, run/revision, target parent, and all ordered accepted-pointer expectations.

create.name: R2 control C6
create.statement: Supply pure expected/actual comparisons and typed diagnostics for every currentness predicate.

create.name: R2 control C7
create.description: Owner `workflowd-vs3.4.3`; phase Before exposure; verification V5
create.statement: Repeat Generation, operation revision, lease, and current-pointer predicates atomically at claim.

create.name: R2 control C8
create.description: Owner `workflowd-vs3.4.4`; phase Before exposure; verification V6; ship after C5/C7
create.statement: Revalidate Workflow, Generation, run/revision, operation, lease, contract, source, and session authority immediately before first task exposure and custody transfer.

create.name: R2 control C9
create.statement: Persist stale/superseded disposition and permit only monotonic current replacement/release.

create.name: R3 control C10
create.statement: Retain ticket revisions by Workflow/hash with Generation foreign-key binding and canonical outer, nested, and source-set hashes.

create.name: R3 control C11
create.statement: On every durable read, decode and recompute outer input, nested request, ordered source set, and ticket semantic identity with exact typed errors.

create.name: R3 control C12
create.description: Owner `workflowd-vs3.4.2`; phase Before exposure; verification V8
create.statement: Fail before task/manifest return on missing, corrupt, or cross-scope authority; never reread tracker, latest path, or technical repository on replay.

create.name: R3 control C13
create.statement: Quarantine/data-error corrupt work and route corrected ticket content through a new hash and successor Generation without mutating old work.

create.name: R4 control C14
create.statement: Enforce finite stage-local Schemas and repository-read, source UTF-8, complete-request, configured-input, result, prompt/launch, and global-result bounds; keep the full ticket out of the request.

create.name: R4 control C15
create.statement: Preserve typed Schema, tag, hash, and size diagnostics through catalog and result decoding.

create.name: R4 control C16
create.description: Owner `workflowd-vs3.4.2`; phase Before exposure; verification V10
create.statement: Return no prepared Document or ImplementationStep until the selected result Schema and every bound pass.

create.name: R4 control C17
create.statement: Permit retry/new revision only with newly valid bounded output; retain failed output as non-advancing evidence.

create.name: R5 control C18
create.statement: Select one retained trusted registration by exact contract ref/hash; keep closures private; reject lookalikes and stage-key dispatch.

create.name: R5 control C19
create.statement: Decode and bound with the selected registration's exact Schemas and repeat compatibility checks on fresh/restart activation.

create.name: R5 control C20
create.description: Owner `workflowd-vs3.4.2`; phase Before exposure; verification V12
create.statement: Invoke only selected assemble/build/prepare closures and return only Schema-decoded prepared output.

create.name: R5 control C21
create.statement: Keep referenced active versions installed and fail closed until the exact registration is restored.
